title: 三只瞎老鼠
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\151-三只瞎老鼠-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Mr. Satterthwaite expresses concern to Mr. Quine that visiting his old friend Tom Addison might be disappointing due to the passage of time, but he insists that Quine accompany him to the Doveton Kingsburn estate. While they are in a shop, they encounter Berilla Gillingham in the china department, who is purchasing colorful tea cups to replace some that broke that morning. Mr. Satterthwaite recognizes Berilla as the widow of Simon Gilliat and the current wife of Tom Addison.

Berilla identifies Mr. Satterthwaite as the guest expected for tea that afternoon at Doveton Grange. After Mr. Satterthwaite apologizes for his delay due to a car breakdown, he offers Berilla a ride to the estate, but she declines, stating she must ride her motorcycle to pick up her children. Mr. Satterthwaite introduces Mr. Quine to Berilla and asks if Tom Addison would welcome an additional guest; Berilla agrees. However, Mr. Quine declines the invitation, citing a prior appointment.

Before departing, Mr. Satterthwaite asks Mr. Quine for an explanation regarding the purpose of their meeting. Mr. Quine provides no direct answer but tells Mr. Satterthwaite that the word "colorblind" might be useful to him. As Mr. Satterthwaite drives toward Doveton Grange, he repeatedly ponders the meaning of that word while reflecting on childhood memories of the estate's lawns, lake, and river. The scene concludes with a description of the tea setting arranged on the lawn, featuring ornate tables, garden chairs with colorful cushions, and a backdrop of a copper beech and a cedar of Lebanon tree.
